item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: The image used in this article is non-compliant with the SCP wiki’s license due to the presence of “NC” in the image’s license. As such, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, done, K_Solari!
Here's what happened to the MTF: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dJzTse9Dsaw

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I critted this before, and I'm glad it's doing great on mainlist!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No one really knows, well, except Dr. Jilani of course :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you! I'm really excited that my first post made it mainlist

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
God, what the hell happened to that cactus?

Anyway, I liked it a lot, it was a nice and interesting short read.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Mind PMing me those errors so I can get them fixed? And thanks for letting me know!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is amusing and a light read, and I'm really glad that you wrote it after all! There are a few capitalization/syntax errors that I think Grammarly would be able to fix, but… man, I just can't see how a three-toed sloth would fit inside that little canary-box.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Great to see the finished product! It's come a long way since the first draft and I had a good few laughs reading this. Love the sloth addition in testing especially. Overall enjoyable read, and a great effort for your first skip!

item.title: My first Skip!
item.content:encoded:
Here we are with my first skip, thank you all for reading! Here's the website and image use guidelines that they provided for the picture: https://group.sciencemuseum.org.uk/creative-commons/. I would also like to thank those who read through and critiqued my work throughout it's writing, Wazikamawata in particular was wonderful in helping me set a proper tone (the sloth is yours buddy).

This licence allows you to:

BY (Attribution): Copy, distribute, display and perform our copyrighted work – and derivative works based upon it – but only if you give us credit in the way we request.
NC (NonCommercial): Copy, distribute, display, and perform our work – and derivative works based upon it – but for non-commercial purposes only.
SA (ShareAlike): Distribute derivative works only under a license identical to the license that governs our work.
